# terrible_community_general_sensu_handler (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) A unique name for the handler. The name cannot contain special characters or spaces.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `command` (String) The handler command to be executed. The event data is passed to the process using STDIN. NOTE: the `command` attribute is only required for Pipe handlers (that is, handlers configured with `type=pipe`).
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter` (String) The Sensu event filter (name) to use when filtering events for the handler.
- `filters` (String) An array of Sensu event filters (names) to use when filtering events for the handler. Each array item must be a string.
- `handle_flapping` (Boolean) If events in the flapping state should be handled.
- `handle_silenced` (Boolean) If events matching one or more silence entries should be handled.
- `handlers` (String) An array of Sensu event handlers (names) to use for events using the handler set. NOTE: the `handlers` attribute is only required for handler sets (that is, handlers configured with `type=set`).
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `mutator` (String) The Sensu event mutator (name) to use to mutate event data for the handler.
- `pipe` (String) The pipe definition scope, used to configure the Sensu transport pipe. NOTE: the `pipe` attribute is only required for Transport handlers (that is, handlers configured with `type=transport`).
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `severities` (String) An array of check result severities the handler handles. NOTE: event resolution bypasses this filtering. Example: [ 'warning', 'critical', 'unknown' ].
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `socket` (String) The socket definition scope, used to configure the TCP/UDP handler socket. NOTE: the `socket` attribute is only required for TCP/UDP handlers (that is, handlers configured with `type=tcp` or `type=udp`).
- `state` (String) Whether the handler should be present or not.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) The handler type.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `config` (String) Effective handler configuration, when state is present.
- `file` (String) Path to the handler configuration file.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
